title: vaccinium oxycoccos subsp. microphyllum
intro: Vaccinium oxycoccos subsp. microphyllum, also known as the small-leaved cranberry, is a subspecies of the cranberry plant that belongs to the heath family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The health benefits of Vaccinium oxycoccos subsp. microphyllum include its ability to help manage urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves and berries can be used fresh or dried for medicinal purposes.
